player_notes.victor wembanyama: In concussion protocol after a hard fall in Game 2 vs. Portland — status for Game 3 unknown, likely OUT or severely restricted. Massive minutes/production void for SAS props.
player_notes.jalen duren: Vowed to be more aggressive after just 4 FGA in Game 1 vs. Orlando — elevated shot volume and scoring expected in Game 2, but Magic defense remains a concern.
player_notes.kevin durant: Cleared and played in Game 2 vs. Lakers after missing Game 1 with knee tendon bruise — monitor knee status heading into Game 3.

game_notes.POR vs SAS: Wembanyama in concussion protocol and likely OUT for Game 3, opening significant role/minutes expansion for remaining Spurs frontcourt players.

suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms Billy Donovan has stepped down as Bulls head coach after 6 seasons. The CHI head coach entry is now stale.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update CHI head coach entry to reflect Donovan's departure. Mark as vacant or interim until a replacement is named. Note CHI is eliminated so playoff impact is nil, but roster/context doc accuracy requires the update.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Durant returned (23/6reb/4ast, 7-12 FG, 41 min) but LAL still won — a more significant result than G1's upset given HOU's full roster was available.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: A news item headlines 'Kevin Durant (knee) cleared to play in Game 2 vs. Lakers' as if it were breaking news, but the Game 2 diary already reflects Durant playing. More importantly, the LAL G2 win score is reported as 101-94 in the news ('LeBron scores 28, leads short-handed Lakers past Durant's Rockets again 101-94') but the context doc records the G2 score as 'LAL 101, HOU 94' — these are consistent. No conflict on score. However, one headline references the Game 2 final as 101-94 while the context doc records it as 101-94 as well. No conflict found on Durant availability or score — this flag is resolved as consistent.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: No change needed on Durant availability or Game 2 score — context doc is already accurate. Confirm the 'Durant cleared' news item predates the game and was already incorporated into the G2 diary entry.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 2 diary notes LeBron as LAL's sole engine; no mention of Marcus Smart playing a featured role. The broader context notes LAL without Doncic and Reaves, with LeBron as sole offensive engine.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Headline reads 'Marcus Smart's breakout game helps Lakers go up 2-0 on Rockets' — Smart guarded Durant and had a breakout performance in G2. This is a meaningful role player development not captured in the context doc's G2 diary, which does not mention Smart at all.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add a note to the LAL vs HOU G2 diary entry that Marcus Smart had a breakout defensive performance guarding Durant. Update the LAL role player context to flag Smart as an elevated contributor in this series, which is relevant for future prop consideration.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Victor Wembanyama (SAS) — Concussion (NBA protocol) — new injury supersedes rib contusion. Took a hard fall in Q2 of G2 vs POR (Apr 21)... No return timeline — must pass all protocol steps before being cleared to play. G3 availability unknown.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news reconfirms Wembanyama is in concussion protocol ('Spurs' Victor Wembanyama in concussion protocol after fall', published Apr 22). Context doc is accurate. Commentary ('Perk: Spurs' panic meter has to be high without Wemby') reinforces no imminent return. No new information on clearance — status remains unknown for G3.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: No change needed — context doc accurately reflects Wembanyama's concussion protocol status. Continue monitoring for any clearance update before G3.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Duren: breakout All-Star season. Averaged 22.6 PPG / 10.7 RPG on 67.8% shooting over final 26 games... Will dominate Wendell Carter Jr. inside — massive size/athleticism advantage.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News headline: 'Jalen Duren vows more aggression after just 4 FGAs in Game 1.' This directly conflicts with the pre-series expectation that Duren would dominate inside. In G1, ORL's defense held Duren to just 4 field goal attempts — he did not dominate as projected. The G1 diary notes 'DET's interior attack (Duren) was not dominant enough' but does not quantify how severely he was suppressed (only 4 FGA).
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update the DET vs ORL G1 diary to specifically note Duren was held to 4 FGA in G1 — a critical suppression data point for G2 props. Flag that ORL's defensive scheme neutralized Duren's interior advantage despite pre-series projections. His G2 props should account for this but also his stated intent to be more aggressive.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Context doc states SGA won back-to-back MVP. 'SGA: back-to-back MVP.'
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News headline: '2026 NBA MVP finalists: Gilgeous-Alexander, Jokic and Wemby' — this suggests the 2025-26 MVP has NOT yet been awarded and SGA is only a finalist, not a confirmed winner. The context doc states 'back-to-back MVP' as fact, which would mean he already won the 2025-26 award. If the award is still being voted on/announced, the context doc is premature in declaring him the winner.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Correct the SGA entry: the 2025-26 MVP has not yet been awarded — SGA is a finalist alongside Jokic and Wembanyama. Remove or qualify the 'back-to-back MVP' language until the award is officially announced. The 2024-25 MVP (confirmed) stands.
